extracted_rules.rule_name: Carbohydrate targets by training day type
extracted_rules.condition: When prescribing daily carbohydrate intake based on training load
extracted_rules.action: Set carbohydrate targets in g/kg body weight based on the day's training type
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Rest day: 3-4 g/kg; light training day: 4-5 g/kg; moderate training day: 5-7 g/kg; hard/long training day: 7-10 g/kg; >2.5h endurance event: 10-12 g/kg
extracted_rules.source: Jeukendrup 2011; Burke et al. 2011; IOC nutrition consensus 2011

extracted_rules.condition: When prescribing daily protein intake for any athlete in active training
extracted_rules.action: Target 1.6-2.2g protein per kg body weight per day; distribute across 4 meals at ~0.4g/kg per meal
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Daily protein: 1.6-2.2 g/kg/day; per meal: 0.4 g/kg (optimal for MPS); minimum 4 meals/day; upper benefit ceiling: ~2.2 g/kg beyond which no additional MPS
extracted_rules.source: Phillips & Van Loon 2011; Morton et al. 2018 meta-analysis; Stokes et al. 2018

extracted_rules.condition: If session duration is 60-150 minutes at moderate-to-high intensity
extracted_rules.action: Consume 30-60g of carbohydrate per hour during exercise; glucose or maltodextrin sources preferred
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: 30-60 g carbs/hour; intestinal absorption rate: ~60g glucose/hour max; sources: glucose, maltodextrin, sports drinks, gels
extracted_rules.source: Jeukendrup 2011 carbohydrate oxidation rates; meta-analysis: carb supplementation and endurance performance (original source)

extracted_rules.condition: If session duration exceeds 150 minutes (2.5 hours) at sustained intensity
extracted_rules.action: Increase carbohydrate intake to 60-90g per hour using a glucose:fructose 2:1 ratio blend to maximize absorption via dual-transporter pathway
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: 60-90 g carbs/hour; glucose:fructose ratio 2:1; dual-transporter ceiling: 90g/h; trained gut required for >80g/h without GI distress

extracted_rules.condition: Before any training session of moderate-to-high intensity
extracted_rules.action: Consume a carbohydrate-rich meal 1-4 hours before exercise; reduce portion size as timing approaches session start
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: 3-4h before: 1-4 g/kg carbs + moderate protein; 1-2h before: 1-2 g/kg carbs, low fat, low fiber; <1h: 0.5-1 g/kg simple carbs only; avoid high-fat/fiber <2h before
extracted_rules.source: Burke et al. 2011; pre-exercise nutrition consensus (original nutrition source)

extracted_rules.condition: After any training session exceeding 60 minutes or high-intensity effort
extracted_rules.action: Consume protein + carbohydrate within 30-45 minutes of session end to maximize glycogen resynthesis and muscle protein synthesis
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Within 30-45 min post-session: 0.3 g/kg protein + 1 g/kg carbohydrate; glycogen resynthesis rate: 5-10% higher with immediate vs delayed intake
extracted_rules.source: Ivy et al. 1988; post-exercise nutrition consensus; Burke et al. 2011

extracted_rules.rule_name: Race-day carbohydrate loading — marathon and long events
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is preparing for a marathon or endurance event lasting >90 minutes
extracted_rules.action: Carbohydrate load in the 24-48 hours before the event; this maximizes muscle glycogen stores
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Carbohydrate loading: 8-12 g/kg/day for 24-48h pre-race; reduce training volume during loading period; muscle glycogen can increase 20-40% above normal
extracted_rules.source: Burke et al. 2011; carbohydrate loading meta-analysis (original nutrition source)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Hydration — pre, during, and post exercise
extracted_rules.condition: During any training session or race
extracted_rules.action: Follow evidence-based hydration protocol across all three phases of exercise
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Pre: 500 ml water 2h before + 250-500 ml 15-30 min before; During: 400-800 ml/h (adjust by sweat rate and heat); Post: 1.5 L fluid per 1 kg body weight lost
extracted_rules.source: ACSM hydration guidelines; Sawka et al. 2007

extracted_rules.rule_name: Glycogen sparing during prolonged exercise with carb intake
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete ingests carbohydrates during a prolonged endurance effort (≥100 min)
extracted_rules.action: Expect reduced net muscle glycogen utilization; this delays fatigue onset and extends sustainable effort duration
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Glycogen sparing: ~24 mmol·kg⁻¹ dry weight during ~100 min exercise with carb ingestion vs. placebo
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: carbohydrate ingestion and net glycogen utilization (original nutrition source)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Low-carbohydrate and ketogenic diet — metabolic adaptation
extracted_rules.condition: If a trained athlete adopts a low-carbohydrate (<130 g/day) or ketogenic (<50 g/day) diet
extracted_rules.action: Expect metabolic fat-oxidation adaptation with maintained or improved aerobic performance outcomes; warn athlete of possible acute performance reduction during 2-4 week adaptation phase
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Low-carb: ≤130 g/day or ≤25% total energy; keto: <50 g/day or <10% total energy; adaptation phase: 2-4 weeks; aerobic performance: maintained or improved after adaptation
extracted_rules.source: Meta-analysis: low-carb and ketogenic diets and aerobic performance (original nutrition source)

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete has a history of GI distress during endurance exercise
extracted_rules.action: Implement gut training protocol; avoid high-fiber, high-fat, and high-fructose foods within 2-3h before and during exercise; gradually increase intra-workout carb intake across training weeks
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Gut training: gradually increase intra-workout carb intake from 30g/h → 60g/h → 90g/h over 4-8 weeks; avoid: high-fiber/fat pre-exercise; trial race nutrition in training
extracted_rules.source: Systematic review: nutritional strategies for minimizing GI symptoms during endurance exercise (original source)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Omega-3 and cardiovascular health for endurance athletes
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete seeks to optimize cardiovascular health markers alongside endurance performance
extracted_rules.action: Include omega-3 fatty acid supplementation; combined with carbohydrate loading, protein optimization, and hydration it produces measurable cardiovascular and performance benefits
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Omega-3 supplementation: reduces resting HR by ~3 bpm, lowers LDL cholesterol; dose: 1-3g EPA+DHA/day; combined nutritional strategy improves endurance by 8-12%
extracted_rules.source: Forensic perspective on nutrition in competitive sports (original nutrition source)
